The '''inchoative''' aspect in Aelaki expresses the beginning of an action or state. It is one of the [[Stative aspects|stative verb aspects]], formed with the prefix '''nü-'''.

== Formation ==

The inchoative is formed by prefixing '''nü-''' to the stative verb stem.

{| class="wikitable"
! Stative verb !! Inchoative !! Meaning
|-
| zoduk (know) || nüzoduk || learn (start to know)
|-
| apafath (fall) || nü'apafath || start falling
|}

== Continuative form ==

The continuative of the inchoative is formed by reduplicating the prefix:

{| class="wikitable"
! Base !! Continuative !! Meaning
|-
| nüzoduk || nünüzoduk || learning (in the process of starting to know)
|}

== Semantic scope ==

The inchoative indicates the onset or inception of a state. With ''zoduk'' "know", the inchoative ''nüzoduk'' means "learn" -- that is, to begin knowing something.

== Relationship to other stative aspects ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Prefix !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| ho- || [[Probable]] || hozoduk || curious (about to know)
|-
| '''nü-''' || '''Inchoative''' || '''nüzoduk''' || '''learn (start to know)'''
|-
| nï- (+ umlaut) || [[Cessative]] || nïzedik || forget (stop knowing)
|-
| lu- || [[Resumptive]] || luzedik || remember (resume knowing)
|-
| ko- || [[Almost]] || kozoduk || almost learn
|}

== See also ==

* [[Cessative]]
* [[Resumptive]]
* [[Almost]]

[[Category:grammatical aspects]]
[[Category:Stative aspects]]
